summary: Phase-2 retest after the 3 missing lifecycle endpoints (move-out, assign-existing, unit-history) were added to /app/backend/routes/landlord_routes.py. Backend regression (test_rentorax_flow.py): 19/19 PASS. Backend Phase-2 (test_phase2.py): 17/18 PASS — the new move-out and assign-existing endpoints both return 200 with correct side-effects (tenant archived/reactivated, unit vacant/occupied, payments preserved), but GET /api/units/{unit_id}/history fails the >=2 entries assertion because it queries db.tenants by unit_id (only 1 row when the same tenant is reassigned to the same unit). Frontend Playwright verified the full move-out happy path through the UI: clicked tenant-moveout-{id}, checked all 5 mo-* checkboxes, clicked mo-confirm, the tenant disappeared from /landlord/tenants default list, PropertyDetail showed unit as Vacant with assign-tenant-{uid} and unit-history-{uid} buttons. assign-existing dialog opens correctly but the 'Archived tenant' picker is a native HTML <select> (not a shadcn Select with data-testid on items), so Playwright text-click resolved to a hidden <option> and the form submitted without a tenant_id — UI assign flow could not be auto-driven. unit-history button is wired and clickable.

minor.issue: Returns only 1 record after move-out + re-assign of same tenant to same unit. Implementation queries db.tenants by unit_id, so it returns the single current tenant row (now reactivated). The spec / test expects each tenancy (prior + current) as a separate entry — should merge db.move_outs (prior tenancies for this unit_id) + the current tenant row, sorted by lease_start desc.

MoveOutChecklist pydantic model accepts only top-level fields (rent_paid, utilities_settled, ...) but the test in test_phase2.py POSTs {"checklist":{...}, "deposit_refund":0, "notes":"ok"} (nested). Because every field has a default, pydantic silently ignores it and returns 200 with an empty checklist persisted. Consider: accept either shape, or document that the body must be flat (which the frontend MoveOutDialog does send correctly).

integration_issues.issue: The Archived-tenant picker is rendered as a native HTML <select> (its <option> elements are hidden in the DOM). Without a data-testid on each option, automated UI tests cannot reliably pick a tenant — Playwright's click on the option text fails ('Element is not visible'). Human users are unaffected; automation is. Replace with shadcn <Select>/SelectItem (each item with data-testid=`assign-tenant-option-{id}`) or use a native select with id+name attributes to permit page.select_option(...).

action_items: Fix GET /api/units/{unit_id}/history in /app/backend/routes/landlord_routes.py to also include past tenancies for the unit (read db.move_outs where unit_id == :id, project each move_out into the same row shape using move_out snapshot fields, then append the current tenant row if status='active'). Sort by lease_start desc / moved_out_at desc. This will flip test_moveout_then_assign_existing_and_history from FAIL to PASS (18/18).
Optional: make MoveOutChecklist tolerant of {checklist:{...}, deposit_refund, notes} payload OR document the flat schema in the API spec.

Optional: replace the native <select> in PropertyDetail Assign-tenant dialog with shadcn Select+SelectItem using data-testid=`assign-tenant-option-{tenant_id}` for each archived tenant, so automation can drive the flow.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/landlord_routes.py L525-542 (unit_history): currently only queries db.tenants — it must union with db.move_outs to surface historical leases. Right now a unit that has had N prior tenants will show only the current one. This contradicts the review_request: 'occupancy history sorted by lease_start desc, with full_name, lease dates, vacated_at, status, archived flag for each prior tenancy.'

MoveOutChecklist (L439-446) silently swallows unknown keys because all fields are optional+defaulted. This caused test_phase2 to send {checklist:{...}} and still get 200 with an empty persisted checklist. Add Config.extra='forbid' or accept a wrapper schema.

assign-existing (L491-522) correctly checks landlord_id ownership and rejects unit-already-occupied with 400 — good. Consider also rejecting when the tenant is not archived (status='active' on a different unit) to prevent accidental relocation.

success_rate.frontend: move-out flow 100%; assign-existing UI-driven flow blocked by native <select> (backend endpoint itself works — verified via test_phase2.py up to L308)

test_credentials: Super admin admin@rentorax.com / Admin@2026 (unchanged). Test landlords are created+cleaned up by tests.
seed_data_creation: test_phase2.py creates 2 throwaway landlords (ph2_ll_* + ph2_lockout_*) and cleans both via DELETE /api/admin/landlords/{id} cascade. Playwright UI test creates ph2ui_* and also cleans it up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only one assertion remains red in the Phase-2 suite: history length. Fix is small (merge db.move_outs into unit_history). Run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://diaspora-homes-dev.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/test_phase2.py -v -n 0` (pytest.ini sets `-n 2` which breaks tests that share STATE). Frontend assign-existing dialog uses native <select>; for automation either switch to shadcn Select+SelectItem with per-item data-testid, or use `page.select_option('[data-testid="assign-tenant-select"]', value=tenant_id)`.
rca of the issue: GET /api/units/{unit_id}/history queries db.tenants {unit_id, landlord_id} only. When the same tenant moves out and is re-assigned to the same unit, db.tenants still holds 1 row for that tenant (reactivated, archived=False), so the endpoint returns 1 entry. The move-out workflow already inserts a snapshot into db.move_outs (L456-466) — these are the prior tenancies. Mitigation: in unit_history, also `db.move_outs.find({landlord_id, unit_id}).sort('moved_out_at', -1)` and append projected rows {id, full_name (from snapshot), lease_start, lease_expiry, vacated_at=moved_out_at, status='vacated', archived=True, amount_paid (from snapshot if stored)} BEFORE / merged-with the current tenant row, then sort by lease_start desc. Also ensure the snapshot in db.move_outs persists lease_start/lease_expiry so prior dates are visible.
